assessment_history.summary: The "backend_foundation" PRD is well-structured, thorough, and clearly written. All four required spec sections (Intent, Goals, Non-Goals, Background/Design Decisions) are present and substantive. The Intent is sharp and scoped. Goals are concrete and enumerable. Non-Goals explicitly defer adjacent concerns to named sibling specs. The PRD goes well beyond the baseline by including detailed Functional Requirements, Technical Boundaries, and Design Decisions — all of which are internally consistent and actionable. Minor observations are noted below, but none block artifact generation.
gaps: Background section is absent as a named heading — contextual motivation is partially covered under 'Design Decisions', but a dedicated Background section explaining the agent-fox platform, the role of af-hub, and why these technology choices were made (e.g., why Go, why SQLite, why TOML) would improve traceability for new contributors.
The 'owner' field in the frontmatter is empty — assigning an owner would clarify accountability.

Admin token validation on subsequent boots requires AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN to be set, but there is no documented mechanism for operators to retrieve the token after the initial boot other than the admin_token file. Edge cases (file deleted, rotated token location) are not addressed.

The `[[auth.oauth]]` configuration block is described under Configuration but OAuth handling is explicitly a Non-Goal for this spec. Including it in the config schema here without further guidance could create confusion about whether the server validates or uses these fields at boot time.
No explicit test strategy or coverage expectations are stated beyond 'make test runs all tests'. Acceptance criteria or minimum test coverage targets are absent.
Error handling behavior for the store layer (e.g., not-found vs. database errors, transaction semantics) is not specified.

The docs/configuration.md reference mentions 'environment variable overrides' but no environment variable overrides are described for any configuration field other than AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN — the scope of env-var support is unclear.

questions.text: Can you add or confirm a Background section explaining the agent-fox platform, what af-hub does within that platform, and why this backend foundation is being built now?
questions.context: A Background section is one of the four standard spec sections. While Design Decisions partially covers the 'why', a brief Background would help new contributors understand the broader product context and motivation.
options: I'll add a Background section describing the platform context.
The Design Decisions section is sufficient — no separate Background needed.
Background will be added in a later revision.

questions.text: Should the `[[auth.oauth]]` configuration block be parsed and validated at boot time in this spec, or should it be loaded but otherwise ignored until spec 02 implements OAuth handling?
questions.context: OAuth provider config is listed under Configuration (this spec) but OAuth handling is a Non-Goal deferred to spec 02. Clarifying whether the server should validate, warn, or silently ignore this block at boot prevents ambiguity for implementers.
options: Parse and validate the structure, but do not act on it (store in config struct only).
Parse with no validation — leave full validation to spec 02.
Omit the oauth config block entirely from this spec's config schema.

questions.text: What is the expected behavior when the admin_token file is missing or has been deleted on a subsequent (non-first) boot?
questions.context: The spec requires AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN on subsequent boots but only describes the file as the delivery mechanism on first boot. If the file is lost, operators need a documented recovery path (e.g., use --reset-admin-token, or re-provide via env var).
options: Document that --reset-admin-token is the recovery path if the file is lost.
The env var AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is the source of truth — the file is informational only.
Add a dedicated recovery/rotation section to the spec.

questions.text: Are there any minimum test coverage targets or specific behaviors that must have unit/integration tests in this spec?
questions.context: The PRD mentions 'make test runs all tests' but does not define what must be tested or to what depth. For a foundational spec, specifying critical test cases (e.g., bootstrap flow, schema init, graceful shutdown) helps ensure quality gates are clear.
options: Define critical test cases explicitly in the spec.
Coverage target is sufficient (e.g., 80% line coverage).
Test strategy will be defined separately — not needed in this PRD.

questions.text: Who is the owner of this spec, and can the 'owner' frontmatter field be populated?
questions.context: The owner field is blank. Assigning an owner clarifies who is responsible for decisions and reviews related to this spec.
